Trademark registration in India is essential to protect your business name, logo, or symbol. It grants you the exclusive legal right to prevent others from using or imitating your brand identity. The process begins with a detailed trademark search to ensure the availability of your name or logo. At BookMyTM, we specialize in assisting you through every step of the trademark registration process across Kerala, Haryana, New Delhi, Rajasthan, and Uttar Pradesh.

Our experienced team helps you file your trademark application with IP India, a division of the Government of India. We handle all the required documentation and guide you in selecting the appropriate trademark class from the 45 available categories. Once the application is filed, we meticulously track the trademark status until you receive your trademark certificate. Your registered trademark will enjoy protection for ten years, with an easy renewal option to maintain its validity.

If you require global brand protection, BookMyTM facilitates international trademark registration under the Madrid Protocol, simplifying the process of securing your trademark in multiple countries. We also manage the Power of Attorney required for trademark filing and provide additional services such as tax filing. Furthermore, you can display the ™ symbol to indicate your brand is protected.
Comprehensive legal protection for your brand, covering goods and services in classes 1–34 and 35–45.

items.a: A trademark is a unique sign, design, or expression which identifies products or services of a particular source from those of others.
It gives you the exclusive legal right to use your brand name, prevents competitors from using a similar mark, and builds brand value.

items.q: What is the difference between TM, SM, and R?
items.a:
TM: Unregistered trademark.
SM: Unregistered service mark.
R: Officially registered trademark.

items.q: What can be trademarked in India?
items.a: Word marks, logos, slogans, sound marks, 3D marks, and even color combinations that distinguish your goods.
Goods and services are classified into 45 classes. You must file under the specific class relevant to your business type.

1. Search
2. Filing (TM-A)
3. Examination
4. Publication
5. Registration (if no opposition).

Valid for 10 years. Renewable indefinitely every 10 years.

Yes, individuals, proprietorships, companies, and LLPs can all register trademarks.
